name: San Francisco Chops
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------SAUCE---------------------------
1 Garlic clove, minced
2 teaspoons Oil
4 tablespoons Dry sherry or broth
4 tablespoons Soy Sauce
1/4 teaspoon Crushed Red Pepper
------------------------FINAL TOUCH------------------------
2 tablespoons Cornstarch
2 tablespoons Water
Preparation Time: 10 minutes
Cooking Time: 40 to 45 minutes
1. Mix sauce ingredients together and set aside.
2. Trim pork chops of fat and sauté in hot oil until brown on both sides.
3. Pour sauce over chops. Cover tightly and simmer over low heat until chops are tender and cooked through, about 30 to 35 minutes. Add a little water (1 to 2 tablespoons) if needed to keep the sauce from cooking down too much. Turn once.
4. Remove chops to platter. Stir in cornstarch dissolved in water. Cook until thickened.
5. Pour over chops and serve.
Tips:
Boneless pork loin chops can be used. Trim fat and pound to 1/4-inch thickness. Cooking takes only 20 minutes.
